titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Panat Family Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday October 6, 2018 at 03:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Correspondence, legal documents, and financial records of the Panat family, owners of estates in the Haute-Garonne in the area around Toulouse. Included are trial records, testaments and genealogies concerning the Panat family and the families to which they were joined by marriage. Prominent among these are the families of de Brunet (later de Brunet de Castelpers), Toulouse-Lautrec, La Rochefoucauld, Saint Chamans and Pujols. On their estates the Panats engaged in animal husbandry and agriculture. They were also influential in the arts, in commerce, and in local and national politics.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of H. B. Kraus in 1963.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Panat Family Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers of the Panat family include correspondence, legal documents, and financial records dating from the 13th to the 19th century. The documents of trials, testaments, and genealogies concern the Panat family and families to which they were joined by marriage. The Panat family lived in the area around Toulouse, France, in the Haute Garonne. They engaged in animal husbandry and agriculture on their estates. The Panats were influential in the arts, in commerce, and in local and national politics.

did.unittitle: Oversize items, including autographs of Guizot, Lamartine, and numerous French kings
